channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Biden promised to turn the page on Trump. Now he's being replaced by him
item.description: Joe Biden staked his presidency on the idea that he was the best person to keep Donald Trump out of power.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: John Ratcliffe, tapped by Trump to lead the CIA, will face questioning in the Senate
item.description: Lawmakers on Capitol Hill will question President-elect Donald Trump's pick to lead the CIA on his vision for America's premier spy agency.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know about Trump's attorney general pick Pam Bondi as she faces questioning on Capitol Hill
item.description: Donald Trump’s pick to lead the Justice Department, Pam Bondi, is set to face questions Wednesday on Capitol Hill over her loyalty to the Republican president-elect, who has vowed to use the agency to pursue revenge on his perceived political enemies.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden moves to lift state sponsor of terrorism designation for Cuba, part of deal to free prisoners
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden notified Congress of his intent to lift the U.S. designation of Cuba as a state sponsor of terrorism, the White House announced, as part of a deal facilitated by the Catholic Church to free political prisoners on the island. Senior U.S.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, COLLEEN LONG, MATTHEW LEE and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden's education chief says he avoided 'culture wars' despite goading from GOP governors
item.description: While Republican states were working to limit school history lessons and ban transgender athletes, President Joe Biden’s education chief said he was focused on what matters — putting more social workers in schools, expanding summer school and building a pipeline of new teachers.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nevada elections leader urges real-time efficiency over changing deadlines
item.description: The influx of mail-in ballots collected on Election Day led to delays in tabulation across Nevada, not the number of ballots received in the days after, Democratic Nevada Secretary of State Cisco Aguilar said ahead of the legislative session.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: GOP Sen. Joni Ernst officially backs Pete Hegseth for DOD after initial uncertainty
item.description: Sen. Joni Ernst revealed she is officially supporting Pete Hegseth for Secretary of Defense after Tuesday's hearing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOJ racing the clock to enshrine 'woke' policing rules, lawyer says, as judge hears Breonna Taylor reform case
item.description: A marathon seven-hour Louisville police reform consent decree hearing did not produce the results the Biden DOJ appeared to hope for, according to one attorney.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP tax leaders: US small businesses could pay more tax than small businesses do in China if Trump cuts expire
item.description: Republican members of the chief House committee on taxes said if Trump's tax cuts expire small businesses could begin paying a higher tax rate than small businesses do in communist China.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Key takeaways from Pete Hegseth's fiery confirmation hearing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pete Hegseth, President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for defense secretary, publicly faced senators for the first time after weeks of questions from Democrats — and praise from Republicans — about his “unconventional” resume.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Republicans pass bill to ban transgender athletes from girls’ sports
item.description: House Republicans passed a bill Tuesday to bar transgender student-athletes from competing in girls’ sports, putting the contentious issue front and center following an election cycle that saw it become a frequent target of Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Michelle Obama to skip Trump inauguration, 11 days after missing Carter funeral
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama will not attend the inauguration of President-elect Trump on Monday, the second time in two weeks that she will be absent from a significant event.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court weighs appeal of ex-Chicago alderman’s corruption conviction
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday signaled it may send a Chicago political scion’s appeal of his conviction for lying to regulators back to a lower court to flesh out the difference between false and misleading statements.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gov. Kathy Hochul delivers State of the State address in NY
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – During her State of the State address, Gov. Kathy Hochul proposed free tuition for older college students, more police on New York City's subways overnight and tax relief for New Yorkers.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Senate GOP incumbents brace for MAGA primary challengers
item.description: Several GOP Senate incumbents are already staring down the threat of primary challenges next year as friction builds between the MAGA and establishment wings. Sen.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: California Democrats approve $50M to fight Trump policies
item.description: One lawmaker said they don't have to choose "between helping LA recover from wildfires and protecting CA from Trump.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: Judge: DFL candidate’s election stands for Shakopee area Minnesota House seat
item.description: State Rep. Brad Tabke, a DFL Minnesota House incumbent who narrowly won reelection, should be able to begin a new term, a judge ruled Tuesday — despite a GOP election challenge centering on a pool of ballots that were cast but never counted.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: READ: Special counsel's final report in Trump Jan. 6 case
item.description: The Justice Department released special counsel Jack Smith's report on President-elect Donald Trump's election interference case, just days before he returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Live updates: Democrats turn up heat on Hegseth as Republican alleges hypocrisy
item.description: Defense Secretary nominee Pete Hegseth appeared before the Senate Armed Services Committee on Tuesday, the first of President-elect Trump's Cabinet candidates to face confirmation.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5

item.title: Schools brace for clash with immigration officials ahead of Trump term
item.description: K-12 schools across the U.S. are gearing up for potential efforts to shield their students as the incoming Trump administration sets its sights on deporting millions of people.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: RFK Jr. faces a complicated confirmation with some opposition on both sides of the aisle
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s views on vaccines and abortion rights are raising alarms across the political spectrum, as he's one of the more controversial picks for the president-elect's Cabinet.
item.dc:creator: Sarah McCammon, MPR News

item.title: David Weiss condemns pardon, defends record in Hunter Biden prosecution report
item.description: Special Counsel David Weiss defended his handling of the Hunter Biden investigation in a final report capping his multi-year investigation into the president’s son.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Cannon allows for release of Smith report on Trump election interference
item.description: U.S. District Judge Aileen Cannon on Monday allowed the release of the volume of special counsel Jack Smith’s report dealing with Donald Trump’s efforts to block the peaceful transfer of power after the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Judge clears the way for release of special counsel Smith's report on Trump's Jan. 6 case
item.description: The Justice Department can publicly release its investigative report on President-elect Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case, a federal judge said Monday — the latest ruling in a court dispute over the highly anticipated document days before Trump is set to take office again.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's inauguration weekend features fireworks, MAGA rally, inaugural balls
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration weekend will start with a fireworks display outside his northern Virginia golf club and end with a trio of inaugural balls where he will speak, the committee planning the events announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Bessent to divest from assets on road to leading Treasury
item.description: President-elect Trump’s Treasury Secretary pick Scott Bessent has plans to divest from funds and other investments.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: One poll finds majority of Greenland respondents support joining US
item.description: A new survey found that a majority of Greenland respondents support joining the U.S. after Trump expressed wanting to take the country over.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: DFL, GOP jockey for power in Minnesota House a day before session’s start
item.description: House Republicans ripped DFLers over tactics that could keep the House from conducting business Tuesday. Melissa Hortman, the House’s top DFLer, defended her moves, telling MPR News that denying a quorum is “the only negotiating power I have right now.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: CA firefighters face impersonators, staffing shortages, low pay
item.description: LA County Sheriff Robert Luna said he encountered a man dressed as a firefighter burglarizing a home.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Steve Bannon says he will ‘take down’ the ‘truly evil’ Elon Musk
item.description: “I will have Elon Musk run out of here by Inauguration Day,” Bannon said, adding “He is a truly evil guy, a very bad guy. I made it my personal thing to take this guy down.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Judge who blocked release of Trump report was 'plainly' wrong, special counsel tells appeals court
item.description: The Justice Department has asked a federal appeals court to move swiftly in reversing a judge’s order that had blocked the agency from releasing any part of special counsel Jack Smith’s investigative report on Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Gov. Hochul defends congestion pricing, explains inflation rebate checks
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – In a few days, New York Gov. Kathy Hochul will deliver her State of the State address.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: President-elect Trump sentenced to unconditional discharge in hush money case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump was sentenced Friday in his hush money case, but the judge declined to impose any punishment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: A sharply divided Supreme Court has rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Pinellas residents struggle with permit delays, seek accountability
item.description: Many Pinellas County residents have been displaced from their homes since Hurricane Helene hit more than 100 days ago. Some have nowhere else to go, forcing them to stay in damaged and unfinished homes until the county gives them the permits to rebuild.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Justice Department says it plans to release only part of special counsel's Trump report for now
item.description: The Justice Department said Wednesday that it will release special counsel Jack Smith’s findings on Donald Trump’s efforts to undo the results of the 2020 presidential election but will keep under wraps for now the rest of the record focused on the president-elect's hoarding of classified documents
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC
